human_verification.notes: Reviewed manifest, authority-source policy, scenario coverage, results, readiness review, deployment notes, and marketing notes for consistency with issue #140 and the repository delivery contract.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to collaboration-native workflow configuration, governance, release readiness, troubleshooting guidance, and artifact production.
Real public authoritative sources are documented in manifest.yaml with publisher, authority reason, review date, refresh interval, decay policy, and current status.

The local package defines collaboration-workflow-specific scenarios, shared-meta-agent request and response contracts, and fallback behavior when delegated outputs are unavailable, stale, or low-confidence.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Best-practice corpus expansion is delegated to #225 with safe fallback to the local collaboration-workflow source baseline.
Scenario normalization, readiness consistency checks, deployment packaging normalization, and commercialization wording review are delegated to #302 through #305 with local-artifact fallback.
Authority-source policy normalization, boundary-routing review, and scaffolding consistency review are delegated to #306 through #308 without weakening the specialist-owned baseline.
notes: Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #140.
Deployment package documents runtime model, tenant isolation, adaptation constraints, monitoring and logging expectations, rollback path, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness notes document buyer profile, packaging, subscription model, proof points, and go-to-market gaps without widening the technical scope.
Validation thresholds and human verification satisfy the machine-checkable requirements in docs/agent-delivery-contract.md.
